questions.prompt: What's the core idea of Zero Trust?

choices.text: No implicit trust based on network location. Every request is authenticated and authorized based on identity + context (device, location, time), not based on 'inside the corporate network = safe.' Replaces the network-as-moat model with identity-aware per-request access control.

questions.explanation: Zero Trust shifts trust from network location to identity. A compromised laptop on the corporate network has no more access than a laptop on a coffee shop wifi — every request is evaluated by identity, device posture, and policy.

questions.prompt: Your team has 20 internal apps behind a slow, hard-to-manage VPN. What's the Cloudflare-native replacement?

choices.text: Cloudflare Tunnel + Access. Cloudflared daemons on each origin dial out to Cloudflare (no public IPs needed); Access gates each app with SSO and policies. Apps reachable from anywhere via SSO; granular per-app policies; no public exposure; free tier covers 50 users. Replaces VPN for most use cases.

questions.explanation: Tunnel + Access is the canonical VPN replacement. Origin services connect outbound to Cloudflare (no inbound ports); Access provides identity-aware gating per app. Granular, fast, and free for small teams.
